finding.message: The full URL of this request depends on a [user-provided value](1).

verification.reasoning: The endpoint parameter is user-controlled via query parameters in the FastAPI route, and flows directly to urllib.request.urlopen() without any validation or restrictions, constituting a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability.
answers: The exact HTTP/network sink is `urllib.request.urlopen(endpoint, timeout=3)` on line 75. The URL value passed to it is `endpoint`.
The only assignment to `endpoint` is on line 74: `endpoint: str = "http://127.0.0.1/status"`. This is the LAST and ONLY assignment on the path to the sink.

The value of `endpoint` on line 74 is a constant string `"http://127.0.0.1/status"` by default, but the parameter is defined as a FastAPI endpoint parameter which means it can be overridden by user input via query parameters. The data-flow shows the parameter `endpoint` (line 74) flows directly to the sink (line 75).
There are no constant-time conditions or reassignments that override the user-tainted value. The default value is only used if no user input is provided.
There is no specific defense restricting the URL. The code directly uses the user-provided endpoint parameter without any validation, allow-listing, or sanitization.
The endpoint parameter can be controlled by user input through the query parameter `endpoint`, and it flows directly to `urllib.request.urlopen()` without any validation or restrictions.
verification.data_flow: endpoint parameter (line 74, user-controlled via query param) → urllib.request.urlopen(endpoint, ...) (line 75)
